source.title: Clinical Management Guideline: Corneal abrasion
source.population: People with suspected corneal abrasion, including trauma- and contact-lens-associated presentations.
source.exclusions: Exact for corneal-abrasion history, symptoms, fluorescein assessment, and referral context; UAE pathways require clinician review.

exact_location.heading: Clinical information and patient summary — causes and symptoms

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports minor-injury, foreign-body and contact-lens context plus pain, blepharospasm, blurred vision, photophobia, watering, and redness.

source.title: Clinical Management Guideline: Corneal abrasion

source.exclusions: Exact for corneal-abrasion history, symptoms, fluorescein assessment, and referral context; UAE pathways require clinician review.

exact_location.heading: Clinical information and patient summary — causes and symptoms

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports minor-injury, foreign-body and contact-lens context plus pain, blepharospasm, blurred vision, photophobia, watering, and redness.

items.rationale: The exact corneal-abrasion features section supports onset, severity, associated symptoms, relevant negatives, risk context and injury mechanism for a possible superficial corneal injury; it does not diagnose an abrasion or cover every eye injury.

items.final_wording: Supports clinician assessment of the involved area, fluorescein documentation, and referral when injury is deeper, contaminated, or possibly infected; medicine details are not mapped.

source.title: Clinical Management Guideline: Corneal abrasion
source.population: People with suspected corneal abrasion, including trauma- and contact-lens-associated presentations.
source.exclusions: Exact for corneal-abrasion history, symptoms, fluorescein assessment, and referral context; UAE pathways require clinician review.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports clinician assessment of the involved area, fluorescein documentation, and referral when injury is deeper, contaminated, or possibly infected; medicine details are not mapped.

items.rationale: The exact assessment and referral section supports recording focused examination, appearance, time-critical features and specialist discussion or escalation only when assessed or clinician-decided.

items.final_wording: Lists severe chemical burns, penetrating injuries, hyphema, embedded corneal foreign body, corneal or lid laceration, blunt trauma, severe abrasion, infective keratitis, and other urgent anterior-eye presentations.

source.title: Guidance for Professional Practice Annex 4: Urgency of referrals table
source.population: People with eye presentations requiring emergency, urgent, or priority referral triage.
source.exclusions: Exact for listed eye red flags and urgency categories; local UAE referral pathways and timing require clinician review.

exact_location.heading: Annex 4 — anterior eye and traumatic red-eye referrals

source.evidence_paraphrase: Lists severe chemical burns, penetrating injuries, hyphema, embedded corneal foreign body, corneal or lid laceration, blunt trauma, severe abrasion, infective keratitis, and other urgent anterior-eye presentations.

items.rationale: The exact anterior-eye and trauma referral table supports documenting traumatic mechanism, urgent features and arranged handover only when identified and acted on by the clinician.

items.final_wording: Lists sudden visual loss, sudden vision change, flashes or floaters with retinal signs, retinal detachment, acute diplopia, severe eye pain with nausea or vomiting, pain on eye movement, and new pupil defects.

source.title: Guidance for Professional Practice Annex 4: Urgency of referrals table
source.population: People with eye presentations requiring emergency, urgent, or priority referral triage.
source.exclusions: Exact for listed eye red flags and urgency categories; local UAE referral pathways and timing require clinician review.

exact_location.heading: Annex 4 — visual loss and other urgent presentations

source.evidence_paraphrase: Lists sudden visual loss, sudden vision change, flashes or floaters with retinal signs, retinal detachment, acute diplopia, severe eye pain with nausea or vomiting, pain on eye movement, and new pupil defects.

items.rationale: Vision loss, severe pain and other urgent eye presentations were reviewed as clinician assessment prompts, never as conclusions.

added_items.rationale: Vision loss, severe pain and other urgent eye presentations were reviewed as clinician assessment prompts, never as conclusions.
